---
title: Timeseries
subtitle: EEG, eye tracking, and other physiological recordings
status: open-design-area
---

BDM does not yet define a schema for timeseries data. This is a deliberate
deferral, not an oversight: existing standards already describe physiological
recordings well, and we would rather point to them than reinvent them.

## What to do today

- Store timeseries recordings as opaque files alongside your BDM dataset
  (e.g., EDF, BIDS-organized EEG, Parquet).
- Use the dataset card to declare that these files exist and where.
- Follow [BIDS](https://bids.neuroimaging.io/) for neuroimaging-style
  recordings and [EDF](https://www.edfplus.info/) for polygraphic signals.

## The open question

The leading candidate design is a thin metadata envelope: a small schema that
describes how an event references a timeseries blob (content type, sampling
rate, channels, units, sample count, offset) while the payload itself stays in
an established format. Follow or join the discussion on
[GitHub](https://github.com/orgs/behaverse/discussions).
